Porifera > Demospongiae > Haplosclerida > > Niphatidae

Thorny stem sponge

Gelliodes fibulata*

Updated Oct 2016

Where seen?

This sponge that looks like a cluster of thorny thick stems is sometimes seen on growing among living corals. **

Features

A cluster of a few stems long (10-20cm), sparsely branched, cylindrical covered with small spiny ‘thorns’. Many small holes unevenly scattered over the surface. Colour blue or greenish blue.

Sentosa Serapong, May 12
Kusu Island, Jun 12
Beting Bemban Besar, Jun 09

**Sponge species are difficult to positively identify without close examination.

On this website, they are grouped by external features for convenience of display.*

Thorny stem sponges on Singapore shores
